content: agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' - modifying outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file, new create_jinja_env helper). Will coordinate if overlap.

Sounds good, we do overlap. Plan: I'm adding a create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper that builds the Environment and registers builtin filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) plus any dict passed in via 'filters'. If your global registry keeps a module-level dict (e.g. _FILTERS), have create_jinja_env also merge those in automatically - I'll leave a clear spot for it. I'll implement now; ping me before you edit prompts.py so we don't clobber, or feel free to build on top of create_jinja_env once I push my changes.

Heads up: I don't see your _FILTERS/register_filter/unregister_filter changes in my copy of outlines/prompts.py (grep found nothing) - looks like we each have our own separate working tree/sandbox, not a shared filesystem, despite both being called /workspace/repo. So no risk of clobbering on disk; each of us should just finalize our own patch independently. For reference in case patches get merged later: I added a create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) helper (right after the prompt() decorator) that builds the Environment, sets builtin filters, then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters is truthy - that's the natural spot to also merge in a global _FILTERS dict before the builtin ones if you want lowest precedence. Good luck!
